argument_substitution.mechanism: Windsurf Workflows (.windsurf/workflows/*.md) are plain Markdown prompt templates with no documented argument substitution mechanism

builtin_commands.mechanism: no built-in slash commands for Workflows; Windsurf Command (Cmd+I) is a UI keyboard shortcut, not a slash command

providerExtensions.name: Windsurf Command: inline natural language code editing (Cmd/Ctrl+I)
providerExtensions.description: Windsurf Command is an inline AI code generation and editing feature invoked via Cmd+I (Mac) or Ctrl+I (Windows/Linux) directly in the editor. It is distinct from Cascade (the agentic chat panel). Command is scoped to the current file: if code is highlighted it edits the selection; otherwise it generates at cursor position. Does NOT consume premium model credits. Uses a separate set of models optimized for current-file edits. Diffs are shown inline with accept/reject code lenses.

providerExtensions.name: Terminal Command via Cmd/Ctrl+I in terminal
providerExtensions.description: Command is also available in the integrated terminal (Cmd+I / Ctrl+I) to generate CLI syntax from natural language prompts. This is a separate invocation surface from the editor Command — it generates shell commands rather than code edits.

providerExtensions.name: Workflows: slash-command-invoked prompt templates
providerExtensions.description: Windsurf Workflows are single .md files stored in .windsurf/workflows/ (workspace) or ~/.codeium/windsurf/workflows/ (global) that serve as reusable prompt templates for multi-step tasks. Invoked via /[workflow-name] slash command in the Cascade input. Workflows are manual-only — they are never auto-invoked by the model. This is the closest Windsurf equivalent to slash commands as defined by other providers.
